sections.paragraph: αἰτέω [etym. complex] ‘ask for in expectation of a response’, ask, ask for, request. Abs. Mt 7:7; Lk 11:9; Js 1:6. W. acc. of pers. approached with a request put in a request to someone, ask someone (for something) Mt 5:42; Ac 13:28. W. acc. of person or thing requested ask for Mt 27:20; Ac 16:29; insist on 1 Cor 1:22. W. double acc. of pers. approached and thing requested ask someone for something Mt 7:9; Mk 10:35; J 16:23. The verb is also used w. various prepositional phrases, as well as one or more of the preceding constructions.
